title: Reflections from the STEP-EA Project: Teacher Well-being in East Africa
excerpt: What does it mean to truly support a teacher in rural Tanzania or Kenya? The STEP-EA project taught me that well-being is not a soft concept — it is the foundation of every effective classroom. Here are some early reflections from the field.

title: Growing Tech Startups in Dar es Salaam: What Silicon Dar Is Getting Right
excerpt: Tanzania's startup ecosystem is maturing. My recent research into Silicon Dar revealed both exciting momentum and structural challenges that need urgent attention from policy makers and investors alike.

title: ICT and Grassroots Entrepreneurship: Evidence from Iringa, Tanzania
excerpt: Our cross-sectional study in Iringa Region found that mobile technology is reshaping how small-scale entrepreneurs connect to markets, access information, and build peer networks — often in ways that formal support structures have overlooked.

title: Completing My PhD: What I Learned About Patience, Purpose, and Research
excerpt: After six years of research, fieldwork, writing, and revising — I defended my dissertation in 2025. Here is a personal reflection on what the journey taught me about academic life, African development, and myself.

title: Why We Still Need NGOs in Innovation Ecosystems
excerpt: When I presented at UNU-WIDER in 2020, I argued that NGOs are essential to ensuring no one is left behind in technology-driven development. Five years later, my conviction has only deepened — and the evidence has grown.
